nodes.label: AI as time-saving tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as threat to educational integrity and teacher autonomy
nodes.grounding_span: Anything I can do to cut down on that time spent while still making sure the students are getting the content they need, I'll do it.

nodes.label: Teacher control over content decisions
nodes.label_negative: AI making decisions about what content is taught
nodes.grounding_span: I don't want AI making decisions about what content is taught.

nodes.label: AI output quality and appropriateness
nodes.label_negative: AI output that is off-grade-level or nonsensical
nodes.grounding_span: Sometimes it doesn't write questions on the correct grade level, or sometimes it will say things that don't make sense at all.

nodes.label: Institutional permission for AI use
nodes.label_negative: Lack of clear district expectations and training
nodes.grounding_span: It would be nice to get some guidance from admin, and also some training or professional development so we aren't guessing about what's appropriate.

nodes.label: AI as tool to address academic dishonesty
nodes.label_negative: Student misuse of AI to bypass learning
nodes.grounding_span: The most frustration comes from when you can tell that students are using AI to complete assignments, such as not reading an assigned book and instead asking AI directly each question and copying the answer.

nodes.label: Work-life balance
nodes.grounding_span: Sometimes I'm up until midnight writing an assignment, or grading, or entering grades into powerschool.

nodes.label: Pedagogical integrity and student understanding
nodes.grounding_span: I don't want AI making decisions about what content is taught.

nodes.label: Institutional support and clarity
nodes.grounding_span: It would be nice to get some guidance from admin, and also some training or professional development so we aren't guessing about what's appropriate.

nodes.label: Student engagement and learning
nodes.grounding_span: students are using AI to complete assignments, such as not reading an assigned book and instead asking AI directly each question and copying the answer.

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for time-saving
nodes.grounding_span: Anything I can do to cut down on that time spent while still making sure the students are getting the content they need, I'll do it.

nodes.label: Frustration with AI inaccuracy in content creation
nodes.grounding_span: I don't want AI making decisions about what content is taught.

nodes.label: Frustration with student AI misuse
nodes.grounding_span: The most frustration comes from when you can tell that students are using AI to complete assignments

nodes.label: Desire for institutional guidance
nodes.grounding_span: It would be nice to get some guidance from admin, and also some training or professional development

nodes.label: Iterative refinement and quality control
nodes.grounding_span: I'll definitely check it and refine it if it isn't what I need.

nodes.label: Proactive self-education seeking
nodes.grounding_span: I guess it's time to seek out books about integrating AI into classrooms
